chunks: Banks and regulated entities never ask customers to share OTPs, PINs, passwords, or full card details over phone, SMS, or email.
KYC updates for bank accounts are done through official bank branches, apps, or verified websites — not through links in unsolicited SMS.
If you receive an urgent message claiming your account will be blocked unless you click a link, treat it as phishing and contact the bank using the number on your card or passbook.
Fraudsters send messages offering instant pre-approved personal loans at unusually low interest rates.
A common advance-fee trick asks victims to pay a refundable processing, insurance, or verification fee via UPI before any loan is disbursed.
Legitimate RBI-regulated lenders do not demand upfront fees through SMS links or personal UPI IDs to release loan amounts.
Real prizes do not require you to pay money, buy gift cards, or pay a registration fee to collect winnings.
Unexpected messages saying you won a lottery or lucky draw you never entered are a classic scam pattern.
Scammers may name real brands or TV shows to sound credible while asking for an upfront fee to release the prize.
YouTube creators do not text random people asking them to subscribe to claim cash prizes.
Fake giveaway scams often misuse famous creator names and include a YouTube link plus a simple action like subscribe or reply.
Verified giveaways are announced on official channels, not through unsolicited SMS or WhatsApp forwards.

source: India Post — parcel and customs fraud
chunks: India Post does not ask recipients to pay customs duty through unofficial third-party payment links in SMS.
Parcel-hold scams invent tracking IDs and threaten destruction or return unless a small fee is paid immediately.
Always verify parcel status on the official India Post website or the retailer you ordered from.
Fraudulent messages claim electricity or gas will be disconnected within hours unless you pay via a link or personal number.
Real utilities send bills through official apps, portals, or printed notices — not threatening same-day cutoff SMS with unknown links.
Do not call personal mobile numbers listed in suspicious utility messages; use the official helpline on your physical bill.
Scammers impersonate real government schemes like PM-KISAN, PMJAY, EPFO, or PMAY and add fake payment or verification steps.
Official benefit credits do not require sharing ATM PIN, full bank passwords, or paying a fee to release installments.
Always use official .gov.in portals — for example pmkisan.gov.in or unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in — not look-alike domains.
The Income Tax Department does not send refund links through unofficial domains asking you to verify PAN within 24 hours.
Real refund status is checked only at incometax.gov.in or the official e-filing portal.
Threats that unclaimed refunds will be forfeited to treasury via SMS links are a known phishing pattern.
Legitimate employers do not charge registration, onboarding, or security deposits before you start work.
Task-job scams on Telegram ask victims to complete a few free tasks then deposit funds to unlock higher-paying combo tasks.
Unsolicited high-salary work-from-home offers that move conversation off email to WhatsApp or Telegram are high-risk.
No bank fraud department will ever ask you to read out an OTP received on your phone to block a transaction.
Caller ID can be spoofed; hearing a familiar bank name on a cold call does not prove the caller is genuine.
If someone claims a suspicious transaction and requests OTP, hang up and call the bank using the number on your card.
Hi Mum/Dad style messages claiming a lost phone and urgent UPI transfer are a common impersonation scam.
Verify through a separate channel — call the family member on their known number before sending money.
Scammers create artificial urgency to stop you from confirming the story.
Delivery-failure scams use look-alike domains instead of the real retailer site and ask for small re-delivery or customs fees.
Unrealistic sale prices with unknown links are used to harvest payment details or install malware.
Track orders only through the app or website where you placed the order.
